steps.title: Connect preposition and object
steps.content: The phrase needs the preposition before the location "Edwards Air Force Base." A preposition should connect directly to its object, so no punctuation should interrupt "at" and the place name.

steps.title: Remove punctuation breaks
steps.content:
• Choice A: A dash after the preposition separates it from its object.
  • Choice B: A comma after the preposition also breaks the connection to the location.
  • Choice C: A colon cannot follow "landed at" because that wording is not an independent clause.
The answer is D.
